text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in our daily lives, questions about ethics are increasingly important. One major concern is bias: if an AI system is trained on data that contains human prejudices, it may make unfair decisions. To ensure fairness, developers must use balanced data and test their models carefully. Privacy is another key issue; AI often needs large amounts of personal information, so protecting this data is essential. Additionally, transparency means that users should understand how an AI reaches its conclusions. For these reasons, responsible AI development requires ongoing effort to build systems that are trustworthy, equitable, and safe for everyone.
